name: Parque Natural del Montgó — Circular desde Denia
region_intro: Mountain biking around Dénia offers diverse landscapes, from mountainous terrain to coastal paths and agricultural routes. The region is characterized by the prominent Montgó Natural Park, which rises over 750 meters, providing extensive trails. Riders can explore Mediterranean forests, rocky sections, and areas with lush vegetation, alongside routes through cultivation fields and along the coastline. This varied topography ensures options for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Dénia The most popular mountain bike trail is Natural Park of La Marjal Pego-Oliva — Circular via the Greenway of Denia, a 25.5 miles (41.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 32 minutes to complete. This moderate route allows cyclists to pedal among rice fields and cane fields. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Parque Natural del Montgó — Circular desde Denia, a difficult 14.0 miles (22.5 km) path. This route winds through Mediterranean forests and rocky sections of the Montgó Natural Park. Local mountain bikers also love the Fishsculpture with a view of the sea – Ancient Olive Tree loop from Urbanización El Greco, a 30.2 miles (48.6 km) trail leading through agricultural areas and past ancient olive trees, often completed in about 3 hours 18 minutes. Mountain biking around Dénia is defined by the Montgó Natural Park, coastal paths, and agricultural routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from relaxed rides to challenging technical trails. The routes in Dénia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1900 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Dénia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Dénia?
A: Dénia offers a wide variety of mountain bike trails, with over 110 routes recorded on komoot. These trails cater to all skill levels, from easy rides through agricultural areas to challenging ascents in the Montgó Natural Park.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels for mountain biking in Dénia?
A: The trails around Dénia are diverse, with approximately 30 easy routes, 40 moderate routes, and 42 difficult routes. This ensures that both beginners and experienced riders can find suitable challenges, from relaxed tours through plantations to technical trails in the Montgó Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Dénia?
A: Dénia's Mediterranean climate makes it an attractive year-round destination for outdoor enthusiasts. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in Dénia?
A: Yes, Dénia features several excellent circular mountain bike routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e925913782/natural-park-of-la-marjal-pego-oliva-circular-via-the-greenway-of-denia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of La Marjal Pego-Oliva — Circular via the Greenway of Denia&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to pedal among rice and cane fields. For a more challenging ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e925861650/parque-natural-del-montgo-circular-desde-denia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural del Montgó — Circular desde Denia&lt;/a&gt;, winding through Mediterranean forests and rocky sections.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike routes in Dénia?
A: Yes, Dénia offers family-friendly cycling routes, particularly those through agricultural areas and along coastal paths like Les Marines. These routes are generally flatter and less technical, providing a relaxed experience for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e925913782/natural-park-of-la-marjal-pego-oliva-circular-via-the-greenway-of-denia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of La Marjal Pego-Oliva — Circular via the Greenway of Denia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that can be enjoyed by families looking for a scenic ride.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Dénia?
A: Dénia's mountain bike trails often pass by stunning natural features. You can explore the diverse landscapes of the Montgó Natural Park, with its Mediterranean forests and panoramic views. The region also features impressive sea caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/68080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Tallada (Cut Cave)&lt;/a&gt;, accessible by coastal paths, and the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya-Segats Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also traverse agricultural areas with orange plantations, offering a different scenic experience.

Q: Are there any permits required for mountain biking in Dénia's natural parks?
A: Generally, specific permits are not required for mountain biking on designated trails within Dénia's natural parks like Montgó or Marjal Pego-Oliva. However, it's always advisable to respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and check for any temporary restrictions or specific rules that might apply to certain areas or times of the year. You can find more information on local regulations via the Dénia tourism office or park visitor centers.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Dénia?
A: Parking availability varies depending on the specific trailhead. For routes starting near Dénia town, you can often find public parking areas. For trails deeper within the Montgó Natural Park or other rural areas, look for designated parking spots near trailheads or small villages. It's recommended to plan your route in advance and check for nearby parking options on maps or local guides.

Q: Is public transport available to access mountain bike trails in Dénia?
A: While Dénia has local bus services, direct public transport access to all mountain bike trailheads can be limited, especially for routes deep within natural parks. Some routes may be accessible from Dénia's urban center, allowing you to cycle directly from town. For more remote starting points, a car or a bike shuttle service might be more convenient. Check local bus routes and schedules for areas like La Xara or Jesús Pobre, which are close to some trail networks.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Dénia?
A: The mountain biking experience in Dénia is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. Riders often praise the region's diverse landscapes, which offer a compelling mix of mountainous terrain, coastal paths, and agricultural routes. The breathtaking panoramic views from Montgó Natural Park and the varied topography suitable for all skill levels are frequently highlighted as key attractions.

Q: Are there cafes or places to eat near mountain bike trails in Dénia?
A: Yes, Dénia and its surrounding villages offer numerous cafes and restaurants where you can refuel. Many routes pass through or near small towns like La Xara or Jesús Pobre, which have local establishments. Along coastal routes, you'll find eateries in areas like Les Rotes. Planning your route with a stop in mind can enhance your biking experience.

Q: Can I rent mountain bikes in Dénia?
A: Yes, Dénia has several bike rental shops that cater to mountain bikers. These shops often offer a range of bikes, including e-bikes, and can provide advice on suitable routes. Some also offer guided mountain bike tours, which can be a great way to explore the region's trails, especially if you're new to the area.
